research_series.series_name: Context Override Rules: Medication, Environment, Behavior, and Health State Inference

patterns.pattern_name: Contextual factors can override or downweight biomarker and wearable inference

mediator.name: measurement_or_biological_context
mediator.pathway: context alters true physiology or measurement reliability
mediator.mechanism: sleep loss, circadian misalignment, heat, pollution, exercise, meal timing, medication or stress can change biomarkers and symptoms independently of target pathology

inference_rule.plain_language_rule: When a strong contextual factor is temporally aligned with a biomarker/symptom change, downweight direct physiological interpretation and require an alternative explanation check.

hypotheses.reason: Sleep/circadian/environmental/behavioral contexts robustly alter biomarkers, wearables, symptoms and cognition.

evidence.summary: Contextual factors can be true causes or measurement confounders; inference should separate biological modification from artifact and downweight overconfident interpretations.

key_sources.title: Contextual Override Rules in Health Inference
key_sources.relevant_finding: Sleep/circadian/environmental contexts have strongest support as override/downweighting factors; medication/substance and menstrual-phase contexts need more direct evidence.
key_sources.limitations: Direct override-rule literature is sparse for some contexts.

limitations: Less direct evidence for menstrual phase and substance-use override rules
Digital models often omit context integration

negative_or_uncertain_findings.reason: Context should usually downweight or redirect interpretation, not automatically negate other branches.

high_value_candidate_nodes.definition: A temporally aligned contextual factor that modifies or confounds interpretation of a biomarker, wearable signal or symptom.

research_gaps: Need high-quality medication/substance/menstrual-phase override studies integrated with wearable and lab data.

most_supported_patterns: Heat and pollution can acutely confound HRV/BP/glucose/CRP.
No single physiological measure detects workload/stress without context.
patterns_requiring_caution: Understudied contexts such as menstrual phase/substance use.
recommended_next_queries: How to operationalize context-aware inference in graph scoring?
summary.overall_assessment: Required override layer for all downstream inference.

works_cited.title: Contextual Override Rules in Health Inference
